svg.text: learn how to live like Jesus taught us by using the spiritual armor God has provided us.
The
following is a summary of what we’ll be learning each quarter:
Quarters 1-3:
The Gospel of John
We learn about the person and work of Jesus as He ministers and preaches in Galilee, Judea,
Samaria, and beyond.
We will highlight the seven signs He performed that point to His deity.
Our main focus throughout will be on the vital importance of a personal relationship with Him for
eternal life.
Quarter 4:
The Armor of God
(Ephesians 6)
We learn about the spiritual armor God has provided for us to live victoriously in spiritual warfare.
We will make each piece of armor as a craft: the Belt of Truth, the Breastplate of Righteousness,
the Shoes of the Gospel, the Shield of Faith, the Helmet of Salvation, and the Sword of the Spirit.
We then conclude the year with a class party.
Memory Verses
We use the English Standard Version (ESV) in class.
We memorize a different passage each quarter:
First Week
: Psalm 139:17 (class verse)
Quarter 1
: Philippians 2:5-11 (Jesus’ Example of Humility)
Quarter 2
: Matthew 5:3-12 (The Beatitudes)
Quarter 3
: Romans 8:31-39 (God’s Everlasting Love)
Quarter 4
: Ephesians 6:10-18 (The Armor of God)
We do go over the verse in class each week and begin memory work, but students will need to
continue to study the verse throughout the week.
The best way to successfully memorize the weekly
verse is to go over it at least once each day.
Waiting until the weekend or on the way to church
Sunday is never a good idea.
Steady, daily practice not only aids long-term memory, but also gives
students the opportunity to apply God’s Word to their lives each day.
In fact, your whole family may
want to memorize the verse together each week.
Every once in a while, I will have a parent ask if their child may memorize the weekly verses in a
different Bible translation or if they can recite verses they learn in school instead.
However, in order
to be fair and consistent, I do require all verses to be recited from the ESV translation and only the
verses assigned in my class.
I have never had a student who put forth the effort not be able to
successfully memorize these verses, even if they have verses they memorize in school as well.
FINAL NOTES
Arrive on Time
Please be sure your child arrives no later than five minutes before class start time (4:55) each
Sunday.
This allows them enough time to complete their missions and get ready for class.
If you
arrive after class has started, your child will have to wait until the end of class to complete their
missions.
Stay Connected!
I do my best to ensure you as parents are connected with what your child is doing and learning in
class:
